questions.questionText: Metformin is the first-line drug for type 2 diabetes because it:
questions.optionB: Decreases hepatic glucose production, improves insulin sensitivity, and does NOT cause hypoglycemia or weight gain
questions.explanation: Metformin: biguanide, first-line for Type 2 DM. Mechanism: (1) Decreases hepatic gluconeogenesis (primary), (2) Increases peripheral glucose uptake (insulin sensitivity), (3) Activates AMPK. Advantages: no hypoglycemia (euglycemic), no weight gain (may cause weight loss), reduces cardiovascular mortality. Side effects: GI (diarrhea, nausea — most common), lactic acidosis (rare but serious — contraindicated in renal failure, hepatic failure, contrast dye use). Vitamin B12 deficiency (long-term). Reference: KD Tripathi — Metformin.

questions.questionText: Insulin lispro is a rapid-acting insulin analog that differs from regular insulin in:
questions.optionB: Faster onset (15 min vs 30 min) and shorter duration — reversal of proline-lysine at B28-B29 reduces self-association
questions.explanation: Insulin lispro: rapid-acting analog (lysine-proline reversal at B28-B29) → reduced self-association → faster absorption. Onset: 15 min, peak: 1-2 hr, duration: 3-5 hr. Given immediately before or after meals (prandial/bolus insulin). Other rapid-acting: aspart, glulisine. Regular insulin: onset 30 min, SC or IV. Intermediate: NPH (12-18 hr). Long-acting: glargine (peakless, ~24 hr), detemir, degludec (>42 hr). Basal-bolus regimen: long-acting (basal) + rapid-acting (meals). Reference: KD Tripathi — Insulin Analogs.

questions.questionText: Levothyroxine (T4) is the drug of choice for:
questions.explanation: Levothyroxine (synthetic T4): drug of choice for ALL forms of hypothyroidism. Oral, once daily (empty stomach, morning). Long half-life (~7 days). T4 is converted to active T3 peripherally (by deiodinases). Monitor: TSH (target normal range). Start low dose in elderly/cardiac patients (risk of angina, arrhythmias). Drug interactions: absorption decreased by calcium, iron, antacids (separate by 4 hours). Antithyroid drugs for hyperthyroidism: methimazole (preferred), PTU (pregnancy first trimester). Reference: KD Tripathi — Levothyroxine.

questions.questionText: Tamoxifen is used in breast cancer as a:
questions.optionB: Selective estrogen receptor modulator (SERM) — antagonist in breast tissue, agonist in bone and endometrium
questions.explanation: Tamoxifen: SERM — tissue-specific estrogen effects. Breast: ANTAGONIST → blocks ER+ breast cancer growth (adjuvant therapy, chemoprevention). Bone: AGONIST → prevents osteoporosis (beneficial in postmenopausal women). Endometrium: AGONIST → increased risk of endometrial cancer and thromboembolism. Used for: ER+ breast cancer (adjuvant × 5-10 years), breast cancer prevention in high-risk women. Raloxifene: SERM — antagonist in breast AND endometrium (no endometrial cancer risk) — used for osteoporosis. Reference: KD Tripathi — Tamoxifen.

questions.questionText: Corticosteroids cause all of the following adverse effects EXCEPT:
questions.explanation: Corticosteroids (prednisone, dexamethasone): cause hyperglycemia (NOT hypoglycemia) — glucocorticoids stimulate gluconeogenesis and cause insulin resistance. Adverse effects of chronic use: Cushing syndrome (moon face, buffalo hump, central obesity), osteoporosis, HPA axis suppression (adrenal atrophy — do not stop abruptly), immunosuppression (increased infection risk), peptic ulcers, hypertension, hyperglycemia/DM, myopathy, thin skin/poor wound healing, cataracts, glaucoma, growth retardation (children). Reference: KD Tripathi — Corticosteroid Side Effects.
